title: Blind Fury!

"'''Blind Fury!'''" is a comic story first published in Marvel UK's [[Star Wars Monthly 159|''Star Wars Monthly'' 159]], written by [[Alan Moore]]. It was later reprinted and colorized in [[Classic Star Wars: Devilworlds 1|''Classic Star Wars: Devilworlds'' 1]].

==Plot summary==
A young [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] trains his [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] skills against a [[Remote/Legends|remote]] while blindfolded, with his [[X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wing]] on automatic pilot. Suddenly, he receives a distress transmission from a woman, only hearing the words '[[Garn/Legends|Garn]]' and 'tower' before the signal degenerates into static.

When he reaches Garn, it appears a bleak world, and he recalls the terror in the woman's voice. Realizing that he has heard of Garn before but not remembering what it was, Luke lands on the planet and observes the tower jutting from the landscape. As Luke enters the tower, the door closes behind him and he is attacked by beasts from the darkness. Despite having him at their mercy, the beasts retreat and allow the shaken Luke to venture deeper into the tower. A man's voice then welcomes him to 'the halls of his damnation'.

Luke soon discovers [[soul snare]]s containing the life forces of previous victims, before coming upon the skeletal remains of [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]] in [[Jedi armor|armor]]. The man speaks again, claiming to be 'the vengeful remains of [[Rur/Legends|Rur]], [[High Shaman of the Terrible Glare]]'. However, Luke remembers that [[Order of the Terrible Glare/Legends|Rur's order]] was supposed to have [[Pius Dea Crusades|been eradicated]] by the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] thousands of years ago, as told to him by [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Ben Kenobi]].

Luke finds Rur's form encased in a transparent pyramidal structure, where he explains that his body is no more and that his mind is encoded and preserved within the 'devices of knowing' before him. His sensors had detected Luke because of his lightsaber use, and had lured him to Garn with the false distress call, as he had done to other Jedi before him. Because Rur had been in the [[Computer/Legends|computer]] for so long, he had lost track of time, believing that the war was only months ago.

Understanding the danger he is in, Luke tells Rur that there are [[Great Jedi Purge/Legends|no Jedi anymore]] and that he got his lightsaber from the [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|last one]]. Thinking Luke is lying, Rur checks his sensor but finds that Luke's pulse has indeed stayed calm. Realizing his vengeance is in vain, he destroys the labyrinth.

While watching the flames for a long time, Luke thinks of hatred and of obsession and blindness. That of Rur and that of his own.
